frameworks.name: Bayesian decision model constrained by efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: rapid efficient representations of ensemble stimuli relative to a dynamic reference

frameworks.name: robust averaging
frameworks.one_line: overweighting ensemble items whose features resemble the reference

core_question: Do two key predictions of an efficient-coding Bayesian model of ensemble discrimination hold — exposure-dependent growth and uniform-distribution attenuation of robust averaging?
key_assumptions: visual system rapidly forms efficient representations of ensemble statistics
encoding is shaped relative to a dynamic reference
average-discrimination decisions follow a Bayesian readout
manipulations: exposure duration to ensemble statistics across three sessions
shape of ensemble orientation distribution (Gaussian vs uniform from oppositely skewed linear components)
ensemble mean offset relative to a variable reference orientation
key_findings: discrimination accuracy improved continuously across sessions
weighting kernel became progressively more non-uniform with prolonged exposure
robust averaging was largely absent under the uniform ensemble distribution despite similar accuracy
interpretations: ensemble representations adapt to recent stimulus statistics in line with efficient coding
robust averaging reflects efficient-coding-shaped priors, not a fixed perceptual heuristic
